title: ceropegia neronis
intro: Ceropegia neronis, also known as the "Horned Milkweed" or "Coral Milkweed", is a succulent plant species native to Madagascar. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Ceropegia neronis include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for treating coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as an infusion or made into a tincture, with some traditional practitioners also using it in poultices to treat skin conditions.
